assertions.description: The Navigation API (window.navigation) is exposed — the surface whose navigation handling this feature refines to ignore in-flight duplicates.
navigation.navigate() is the method that starts a navigation; a rapid same-URL duplicate call is what Chrome 151 now ignores instead of aborting the first.
NavigateEvent is the event fired for each navigation; the demo uses it to hold a navigation in flight so a duplicate can be observed.
NavigateEvent.intercept() lets a page take over a same-document navigation and keep it in flight — the mechanism that makes the duplicate-vs-ignore behavior observable.
